title: Find and investigate your first insight

blocks.content:
# Find and investigate your first insight

This guide shows you how to quickly identify and investigate a service issue using Grafana Cloud Knowledge Graph.

You'll learn to:

- Navigate to the knowledge graph
- Understand insight rings
- Filter for critical issues
- Investigate an entity's health

This takes about 3-5 minutes.

## Navigate to the entity catalog

The knowledge graph automatically discovers your services and infrastructure, generates insights about issues, and connects metrics, logs, and traces in a unified workflow.

You can view these entities and relations in the entity catalog.
Open the entity catalog from the navigation menu.

steps.description: Navigate to **Entity catalog** in the menu

blocks.content:
## Understand insight rings

Each entity displays two colored rings showing its health status:

- **Outer ring**: Insights directly on this entity (red=critical, yellow=warning, blue=info)
- **Inner ring**: Insights propagated from entities in hierarchical relationships (for example, pods controlled by a service)

The ring colors help you quickly identify what needs attention.

## Find an entity with issues

You can find entities that need attention in two ways:

- **Visually**: Look for entities with red or yellow rings
- **Use filters**: Filter by insight category (Error, Failure, Saturation, etc.) to narrow down the view

For this guide, you can simply look for any entity with a red ring.

## Select an entity to investigate

Click on an entity name with a red ring to view its details. The rings show that the knowledge graph has detected issues.

## Investigate with RED metrics

The service overview displays RED metrics (Request rate, Error rate, Duration) that help you understand the health and performance of the service. The colored rings you saw indicate issues detected by the knowledge graph. These metrics show you the impact. Look for spikes, drops, or patterns that explain the red ring.

## Next steps

You've learned how to find and investigate issues using the knowledge graph!

**What the rings mean**

- Red rings indicate that the knowledge graph detected critical issues (errors, failures, saturation). 
- Yellow rings show warnings that may need investigation.
- Blue rings are informational (deployments, configuration changes).

**From here you can**

- Click **View** to see the entity graph and relationships with upstream and downstream services
- Switch to the **Logs**, **Traces**, or **Profiles** tabs for deeper analysis
- Click **Add to RCA Workbench** to investigate incidents involving multiple components
- Check **Operations** and **Resources** sections below for more detailed metrics

For more information, refer to the [knowledge graph documentation](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/knowledge-graph/).
